children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the President shall impose the sanctions described in subsection (c) with respect to each foreign person that the President determines, on or after , engages in an activity described in subsection (b).December 18, 2025December 18, 2025
undertaken actions or policies that threaten the peace, security, stability, or territorial integrity of any area or state in the Western Balkans;
engaged or attempted to engage in actions or policies that undermine democratic processes or institutions in the Western Balkans;

engaged or attempted to engage in corruption related to the Western Balkans, including corruption by or on behalf of, a government in the Western Balkans, or a current or former government official in the Western Balkans;
engaged or attempting to engage in serious human rights abuses in the Western Balkans; or

engaged or attempted to engage in, acts that obstruct or threaten the implementation of any regional security, peace, cooperation, or mutual recognition agreement or framework or accountability mechanism related to the Western Balkans, including the Prespa Agreement of 2018; the Ohrid Framework Agreement of 2001; United Nations Security Council Resolution 1244; the Dayton Accords; or the Conclusions of the Peace Implementation Conference Council held in London in December 1995, including the decisions or conclusions of the High Representative, the Peace Implementation Council, or its Steering Board; or the International Criminal Tribunal for the former Yugoslavia, or, with respect to the former Yugoslavia, the International Residual Mechanism for Criminal Tribunals.

children.heading: Blocking of property
children.label: 1 Blocking of property
children.text: The President may exercise all authorities granted under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.) to the extent necessary to block and prohibit all transactions in property and interests in property of the foreign person if such property and interests in property are in the United States, come within the United States, or come within the possession or control of a United States person.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Ineligibility for visas, admission, or parole
children.label: 2 Ineligibility for visas, admission, or parole
i
children.text: inadmissible to the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: ineligible to receive a visa or other documentation to enter the United States; and

children.label: iii
children.text: otherwise ineligible to be admitted or paroled into the United States or to receive any other benefit under the Immigration and Nationality Act ( et 16 seq.).8 U.S.C. 11011So in original.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The visa or other entry documentation of any alien described in subsection (a) is subject to revocation regardless of the issue date of the visa or other entry documentation.

children.label: I
children.text: take effect immediately; and

children.label: II
children.text: cancel any other valid visa or entry documentation that is in the possession of the alien.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 1 Implementation
children.text: The President may exercise all authorities provided under sections 1702 and 1704 of title 50 for purposes of carrying out this section.

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: 2 Penalties
children.text: The penalties provided for in subsections (b) and (c) of shall apply to a person that violates, attempts to violate, conspires to violate, or causes a violation of this section or any regulations promulgated to carry out this section to the same extent that such penalties apply to a person that commits an unlawful act described in .section 1705 of title 50section 1705(a) of title 50

children.heading: Agricultural commodity
children.label: 1 Agricultural commodity
children.text: The term “agricultural commodity” has the meaning given such term in .section 5602 of title 7

children.heading: Medical device
children.label: 2 Medical device
children.text: The term “medical device” has the meaning given the term “device” in .section 321 of title 21

children.heading: Medicine
children.label: 3 Medicine
children.text: The term “medicine” has the meaning given the term “drug” in .section 321 of title 21

children.heading: Foreign person
children.label: 4 Foreign person
children.text: The term “foreign person” means a person that is not a United States person.
the person is not engaging in the activity that was the basis for such sanction or has taken significant verifiable steps toward stopping such activity; and
the President has received reliable assurances that the person will not knowingly engage in activity subject to such sanction in the future.

children.label: A
children.text: determines that such a waiver is in the national security interests of the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: not less than 15 days before the granting of the waiver, submits to the appropriate committees of Congress a notice of and justification for the waiver.

children.heading: Form
children.label: 2 Form
children.text: The waiver described in paragraph (1) may be transmitted in classified form.

children.label: A
children.text: the conduct or facilitation of a transaction for the provision of agricultural commodities, food, medicine, medical devices, humanitarian assistance, or for humanitarian purposes; or

children.label: B
children.text: transactions that are necessary for, or ordinarily incident to, the activities described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Compliance with international obligations and law enforcement activities
children.label: 2 Compliance with international obligations and law enforcement activities
i
children.text: the Agreement between the United Nations and the United States of America regarding the Headquarters of the United Nations, signed at Lake Success , and entered into force ;June 26, 1947November 21, 1947

children.label: ii
children.text: the Convention on Consular Relations, done at Vienna , and entered into force ; orApril 24, 1963March 19, 1967

children.label: iii
children.text: any other international agreement; or

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out or assist law enforcement activity in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: any activity subject to the reporting requirements under title V of the National Security Act of 1947 ( et seq.); or50 U.S.C. 3091

children.label: B
children.text: any authorized intelligence activities of the United States.

children.heading: Exception relating to importation of goods
children.label: 4 Exception relating to importation of goods

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The requirement to block and prohibit all transactions in all property and interests in property under this section shall not include the authority or a requirement to impose sanctions on the importation of goods.

children.heading: Defined term
children.label: B Defined term
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “good” means any article, natural or manmade substance, material, supply or manufactured product, including inspection and test equipment, and excluding technical data.

children.heading: Rulemaking
children.label: i Rulemaking
children.text: The President is authorized to promulgate such rules and regulations as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section (which may include regulatory exceptions), including under section1704 of title 50).
Nothing in this section may be construed to limit the authorities of the President under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.).50 U.S.C. 1701
Nothing in this section shall be construed to modify any sanctions in effect as of .December 18, 2025

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: k Sunset
children.text: This section shall cease to have force or effect beginning on the date that is 8 years after .December 18, 2025
